note: All numbers from Bitcoin Core bench_bitcoin binary — real Bitcoin Core validation pipeline. NOT bench_unified. Medians of ≥3 stable runs; outliers excluded when CPU thermal spike detected (>10% deviation).

results_nolto.note: Release without LTO. SECP256K1_CORE_BACKEND_MODE=ON: compiled-in w=8 table (no file I/O). Medians ≥3 stable runs (i5-14400F, performance governor, turbo off, taskset -c 0).
results_nolto.build: SECP256K1_BUILD_SHIM=ON + SECP256K1_CORE_BACKEND_MODE=ON (shim+static table compiled into engine, no external files)

test_suite.note: 749/749 Bitcoin Core unit tests pass with Ultra backend

nolto_gap_root_cause.finding: ConnectBlock -3% without LTO is caused by ultra's larger instruction footprint, NOT by secp256k1 operation latency.
evidence: VerifyScriptP2WPKH (full verify path): ultra 45.5µs ≈ libsecp 45.1µs → secp256k1 ops at parity
VerifyScriptP2TR_KeyPath: ultra 44.5µs vs libsecp 45.7µs → ultra 1.03x FASTER
VerifyScriptP2TR_ScriptPath: ultra 64.6µs vs libsecp 83.0µs → ultra 1.28x FASTER
SECP256K1_UNITY_BUILD=ON (single-TU compilation): ConnectBlock ratio unchanged at 0.97
-ffunction-sections + --gc-sections: removes <1% of ultra binary, gap unchanged
ultra .text section: 10.5 MB vs libsecp .text: 9.6 MB (difference: ~900 KB)
ultra secp256k1 symbols: ~1.3 MB (853 symbols) vs libsecp ~400 KB
largest ultra symbols: scalar_mul_jac (72 KB), dual_scalar_mul (52 KB), ct::generator_mul (49 KB) — ALL on hot verify/sign path, none dead
With LTO: ConnectBlock ratio = 1.00 (global layout optimization eliminates cache pressure)
nolto_gap_root_cause.root_cause: Ultra's more sophisticated algorithms (GLV endomorphism, FieldElement52, Jacobian52, CT paths, wNAF) produce ~1.3 MB of secp256k1 code vs libsecp's ~400 KB minimal implementation. Without LTO, this extra 900 KB competes with Bitcoin Core's hot functions (CCheckQueue, UTXO loop) for L2/L3 instruction cache across 2500 ConnectBlock iterations. Every byte of the extra code IS used — it is the foundation of ultra's signing speedups (14-32%) and algorithmic improvements. LTO eliminates the gap by co-optimizing code layout globally.
nolto_gap_root_cause.not_fixable_without: LTO (which already works) — the extra code is intentional and necessary for the algorithm speedups
nolto_gap_root_cause.recommendation: Use LTO for production builds (-DCMAKE_INTERPROCEDURAL_OPTIMIZATION=ON). ConnectBlock parity is confirmed with LTO. For non-LTO builds, the 3% gap is a known trade-off of richer algorithms: signing 14-32% faster, verify paths equal or faster.

known_limitations: ConnectBlock -2.4% without LTO: ultra's verify path uses more sophisticated algorithms (GLV, FieldElement52, Jacobian52, CT) than libsecp's minimal wNAF. More complex code/data access patterns without LTO's global layout optimization. File I/O overhead resolved (static compiled-in w=8 table). LTO eliminates the gap (ratio=1.00).
BIP324 ECDH: 2% within noise margin (no optimized CT ECDH for this path yet)
